title: Explicit 2025 Monaco and Rule-Pressure Features, Not Just Generic Dry-Compound Compliance
summary: Your current best already tests the normal two-dry-compound rule, but 2025 Monaco changed materially: drivers must use at least three tyre sets there, and wet/intermediate usage changes the obligation structure.
rationale: Your current best already tests the normal two-dry-compound rule, but 2025 Monaco changed materially: drivers must use at least three tyre sets there, and wet/intermediate usage changes the obligation structure. That is a concrete, dated regime shift likely to matter in 2025 rows.
implementation_hint: Add rule-state features such as `Required_Stops_Remaining`, `Sets_Used_So_Far`, `Dry_Compounds_Used_So_Far`, `Monaco_2025_Special_Rule`, `Rule_Pressure_Per_Lap_Left`, and wet-exemption flags. Gate them by `Race`, `Year`, and current wet/dry compound regime.
expected_effect: Likely to help mainly on Monaco 2025 and other rule-pressure edge cases, improving robustness more than average CV.
risk: The gain may be narrow if Monaco rows are a small share; incorrect interpretation of sets/stops from available columns could create mislabeled rule state.
